title: Binary Search

content.problemStatement:
Complete the code for Binary Search to find the index of an element KKK in the given array AAA.
Output the index of the element of KKK, if KKK is present in AAA, else output −1-1−1.
constraints: Array elements are distinct and sorted in ascending order
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers n and k — the size of the array and the target value. The second line contains n distinct integers in strictly increasing order, separated by single spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the 0‑based index of k in the array if it exists, otherwise -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: 7 is at index 3 (0‑based). Second test case: 2 does not appear in the array, so the answer is -1.
The target 5 is the last element, its 0‑based index is 4.
10 is not present in the array, therefore output -1.

solution.approach: Because the array is sorted and contains distinct elements, binary search can locate the target in logarithmic time. We maintain two pointers, left and right, and repeatedly examine the middle element. If it matches the target we return its index; otherwise we discard the half in which the target cannot lie. This yields O(log n) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log n) per test case, O(T·log n) total
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space besides the input array
